title: Shared horizontal plane with staggered vertical turns
summary: Two fiber strands share the same horizontal plane and route in the same direction without stacking. Each takes its own vertical lane and turns at staggered X before horizontal segments would overlap.
visually_observable_good_behavior: Two strands share a horizontal run without collapsing into the same lane.
Each strand turns vertical at a distinct X before paths would overlap.
Horizontal segments remain separated and traceable.
Staggered turn order prevents coincident vertical lanes.

likely_followed_rules.rule_name: Fiber Strand Overlap, Crossing, and Collision
followed_requirements: Two strands must not share the same horizontal or vertical route lane.
Routes must use separate lanes when separate lanes are available.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: Staggered vertical turns assign distinct lanes before horizontal overlap would occur.

followed_requirements: Related strands use adjacent routing lanes as an organized group.
Lane bands preserve readable separation while keeping related strands nearby.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: The pair stays visually related while each retains its own lane through the turn.

legacy_rule_mapping.note: Migrated from docs/reference/routing-examples/; legacy R-labels superseded by SDC IDs above.

notes: Which line turns first is unconstrained as long as lane separation holds.
